headings.text: Why Are Regular Dental Exams in Bellevue WA Important?
What Happens During a Dental Exam at AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry?
Who Should Get Dental Exams—and How Often?
What About Dental Exams for Kids?
Take Control of Your Oral Health at AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry

paragraphs: Maintaining a healthy smile starts with regular dental exams in Bellevue WA. At AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry we believe prevention is the key to good oral health. Whether you're coming in for your routine checkup or have a specific concern, our team is here to ensure your teeth and gums stay in top shape. But what exactly happens during a dental exam, and why is it so important? Let’s walk you through it.

You’ve probably heard it before: regular dental exams are essential for good oral health. But why? Think of a dental exam as a routine check-up for your teeth—similar to how you’d visit a doctor for your overall health. During these visits, we can catch issues early, saving you from potential pain, tooth loss, or expensive procedures down the road.

Oral health is connected to overall health. Conditions like gum disease can increase your risk for heart disease and diabetes, while missing teeth can lead to difficulties eating or speaking. We want to help you avoid these problems with regular, proactive care.
A typical dental exam includes three main components: a professional dental cleaning, a thorough examination, and sometimes X-rays. Let’s break it down further:
One of our skilled dental hygienists will start by giving your teeth a professional cleaning. Here’s what’s involved:
After the cleaning, one of our experienced dentists will examine your teeth, gums, and mouth to check for:
Our goal is to spot any issues early when they’re easier (and less expensive) to treat. If we detect a problem, we’ll walk you through your treatment options.

Depending on your oral health and when you last had X-rays, we may take images of your teeth and jaw. X-rays help us see issues that aren’t visible to the naked eye, like cavities hiding between teeth, bone loss, or impacted teeth. If you’re concerned about radiation, don’t worry—modern dental X-rays use a very low dose of radiation, and we take every precaution to keep you safe.
Most adults and children should have a dental exam every six months. However, some people may need to come in more frequently, especially if you:
Not sure how often you need to come in? Our team will help create a personalized plan for your oral health based on your individual needs.

Yes, even little ones need dental exams! In fact, we recommend bringing your child in for their first dental exam around their first birthday, or within six months of their first tooth coming in. Establishing good habits early on sets them up for a lifetime of healthy smiles.
We offer pediatric dental exams with a gentle, kid-friendly approach. Our goal is to make your child feel comfortable and safe while teaching them the importance of good oral care.

Do Dental Exams Hurt?
We understand some patients feel nervous about going to the dentist, especially if they’ve had a bad experience in the past. The good news? Most dental exams are completely painless. The cleaning might feel a bit uncomfortable if there’s a lot of tartar buildup, but it shouldn’t be painful. If you're anxious, just let us know! We can offer solutions to make your visit as relaxing as possible.

Are X-Rays Safe?
Dental X-rays are considered very safe. The amount of radiation you’re exposed to during a dental X-ray is extremely low. However, if you're pregnant or think you might be, be sure to let us know in advance so we can take extra precautions.

There’s not much you need to do before a dental exam. If you take any medications or have certain health conditions—like heart issues or immune disorders—let us know. Some patients may need to take antibiotics before their exam to reduce the risk of infection. Not sure if this applies to you? We’ll walk you through everything when you book your appointment.

Your smile is one of your most valuable assets, and regular dental exams are key to keeping it bright, healthy, and pain-free. At AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry in Bellevue WA, our dedicated team is here to make your visits comfortable and stress-free.
Ready to schedule your next dental exam? Contact us today to book your appointment. We look forward to helping you maintain a healthy, beautiful smile for years to come!

AnaBella Family and Cosmetic Dentistry
11216 NE 15th St Suite A
Bellevue WA 98004
(425) 454-3221

9:00am - 5:00pm

9:00am - 5:00pm
